2606.25645.title: Taxonomy of Risks on Automated Fact-Checking Systems Considering its Propagation
2606.25645.paper_summary: In recent years, the posting of fake news including disinformation and misinformation on social networking services (SNS) has become a social problem. To combat this fake news, fact-checking that is the process of assessing the veracity of posts on SNS has become increasingly important. While fact-checking is currently performed by fact-checking organizations, it is difficult to fact-check all posts on SNS. Therefore, the use of automated fact-checking systems is effective. Recent automated fact-checking systems utilize artificial intelligence and large language models, so there are risks of incorrect judgments and posting incorrect results on social media which can lead to the spread of misinformation or to engage in defamation. In this paper, as a first step toward enabling the safe use of automated fact-checking systems, we categorize the specific risks on automated fact-checking systems. In this categorizing, we consider a three-stage risk propagation: risk factors, hazardous situations, and harm. Our analysis revealed that 32 specific risks exist in automated fact-checking systems. In this paper, we utilize the categorized risks as analytical cues (guide words) to present the risk assessment of the automated fact-checking system DEFAME. This assessment result indicates that risks that cannot be derived using STRIDE, a conventional IT security risk assessment method can be derived using our guide words.
2606.25645.paper_summary_zh: 近年來，社交網路服務（SNS）上發布假新聞，包括虛假資訊和誤導性資訊，已成為一個社會問題。為了對抗這些假新聞，事實查核，即評估SNS上帖子真實性的過程，變得越來越重要。雖然目前的事實查核是由事實查核組織執行，但對所有SNS上的帖子進行事實查核是困難的。因此，使用自動化事實查核系統是有效的。最近的自動化事實查核系統利用人工智慧和大型語言模型，因此存在錯誤判斷的風險，以及在社交媒體上發布錯誤結果的風險，這可能導致誤導性資訊的擴散或涉及誹謗。在本文中，作為實現自動化事實查核系統安全使用的第一步，我們對自動化事實查核系統的具體風險進行分類。在這一分類中，我們考慮了三階段的風險傳播：風險因素、危險情況和損害。我們的分析顯示，自動化事實查核系統中存在32個具體風險。在本文中，我們利用這些分類風險作為分析線索（指導詞），來呈現自動化事實查核系統DEFAME的風險評估。這一評估結果表明，無法通過STRIDE這一傳統IT安全風險評估方法推導出的風險，可以通過我們的指導詞推導出來。
2606.25645.author: Jun Yajima et.al.
2606.25645.authors: Jun Yajima, Tatsuya Oka, Takao Okubo
